topWin: The broad local Chromium verification path went from noisy timeout territory to a clean 86/86 pass, which makes future browser regressions far easier to trust and diagnose.
topGap: The next leverage is user-facing again: deeper proof/outcome/objection handling on the core conversion pages now that the delivery and verification path is stable.
ignisNote: The right way to finish the shell hardening work was to remove noisy local verification debt too, because reliable browser signal is part of the product quality bar, not a separate concern.
